statement.p: One of the tires on your car looks a little flat. You measure its air pressure and are alarmed to see it so low at {23\ {\rm psi}}\text{.} You have a portable device that can pump air into the tire increasing the pressure at a rate of {1.9\ {\textstyle\frac{\rm\mathstrut psi}{\rm\mathstrut min}}}\text{.} How long will it take to fill the tire to the manuals recommended pressure of {30\ {\rm psi}}\text{?} Write an equation to model this scenario. There is no need to solve it.

solution.p: This is a rate exercise, and we can use

In this case, the initial value for the pressure is 23\text{,} the pressure is increasing (so we will add, not subtract), the rate is 1.9\text{,} and the final pressure value is 30\text{.} The one thing we do not know is how much time it will take to fill the tire, so we decide to use t to represent that time. So the equation to set up is:
